content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Supreme Court of California 
Laurence P. Gill 
Clerk of the Supreme Court 
4250 State Building 
San Francisco, CA 94102 
Dear Larry: 
October 29, 1985 
This letter will confirm our telephone conversation of 
today about the requirement for Justice Kaus to file a 
Leaving Office Statement of Economic Interests. 
You 
indicated that Justice Kaus resigned as a full time member 
of the Court approximately a week ago and immediately began 
to serve as a pro tern judge, which he \vill continue to do 
until sometime in December. 
I advised you that since there 
was no break in service, Justice Kaus is not required to file 
a Leaving Office Statement now, but will be required to file 
within 30 days of the date that he leaves the position of 
pro tern judge (Gov. Code Section 87205 and FPPC Regulation 
18724). 
If you have any additional questions, please feel free 
to call me at (916) 322-5662. 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Alice Hughes 
Documents Examiner 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660

sections.conclusion: Since there was no break in service, Justice Kaus is not required to file a Leaving Office Statement now, but will be required to file within 30 days of the date that he leaves the position of pro tern judge (Gov. Code Section 87205 and FPPC Regulation 18724).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Justice Kaus must file a Leaving Office Statement of Economic Interests upon resigning from his position as a full-time Supreme Court member to become a pro tem judge.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No Leaving Office Statement is required at this time because Justice Kaus's service as a pro tem judge constitutes a continuation of judicial service. A statement will be required within 30 days of leaving the pro tem position.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections; content derived from letter body confirming prior telephone advice. Document format is informal confirmation letter rather than formal advice letter template.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Justice Kaus must file a Leaving Office Statement of Economic Interests upon resigning from his position as a full-time Supreme Court member to become a pro tem judge.

CONCLUSION: Since there was no break in service, Justice Kaus is not required to file a Leaving Office Statement now, but will be required to file within 30 days of the date that he leaves the position of pro tern judge (Gov. Code Section 87205 and FPPC Regulation 18724).

You indicated that Justice Kaus resigned as a full time member of the Court approximately a week ago and immediately began to serve as a pro tern judge, which he \vill continue to do until sometime in December. I advised you that since there was no break in service, Justice Kaus is not required to file a Leaving Office Statement now, but will be required to file within 30 days of the date that he leaves the position of pro tern judge (Gov. embedding.summary: FPPC advises the Supreme Court Clerk that Justice Kaus need not file a Leaving Office Statement upon transitioning from full-time to pro tem judge service, but must file one within 30 days of completely leaving judicial office.
